target.title: feat(zalouser): surface inbound photo attachments via media array
target.body:
## Summary

zca-js already delivers photo content via `TAttachmentContent.href` (see [`src/models/Message.ts`](https://github.com/RFS-ADRENO/zca-js/blob/main/src/models/Message.ts) in RFS-ADRENO/zca-js), but the zalouser plugin layer discards it - `normalizeMessageContent` flattens the object to a JSON-string fallback, so the agent only sees the photo URL embedded as text.

In practice (production deployment on Qwen3.6-35B servicing Vietnamese customer support over Zalo), models that recognize the URL-in-text call the dynamic `image` tool unreliably (~50% hit rate), and when they do call it the round-trip adds ~5s latency just to retrieve content that was already in the message.

This change wires inbound photos into the same channel media pipeline Telegram and Discord extensions already use (`saveRemoteMedia` -> `toInboundMediaFacts` -> `media: [...]` array on `buildContext`). The result: the photo arrives at the LLM as a native vision content block in the user turn, identical to how Telegram and Discord photo messages present today. No tool call required.

## What changes

- **New type** `ZaloInboundMedia` (`{kind: "image", url, thumbUrl?}`) in `types.ts`.
- **New helper** `extractInboundMedia` in `zalo-js.ts` detects photo content via:
  - `content.type === "photo"` (explicit), OR
  - image file extension in href (`.jpg/.jpeg/.png/.gif/.webp/.bmp`, case-insensitive, query-string-tolerant), OR
  - presence of a `thumb` URL (zca-js only ships `thumb` on photos).

  Returns `null` for text, link previews, files, stickers, audio - those continue through the existing `normalizeMessageContent` text path unchanged.
- `toInboundMessage` attaches the resolved media to the returned `ZaloInboundMessage`.
- `monitor.ts processMessage`:
  - Downloads the photo via `saveRemoteMedia` (mirrors `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-media.ts` pattern).
  - Passes the saved file as a top-level `media: [...]` array to `buildContext` via `toInboundMediaFacts`.
  - Relaxed early-return: photo-only messages (no text caption) are now processed; previous code returned early when `rawBody` was empty, silently dropping photo-only inbound messages.

## Real behavior proof

After-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup (DOS production deployment, container `agent-customer-service-25976c` running OpenClaw 2026.5.18 with this patch ported to the bundled dist, model `dos-ai/dos-ai` = Qwen3.6-35B-a3b).

**Setup:** Vietnamese customer-support agent "Em Huyền" serving Tingee merchants over Zalo personal account (zalouser channel). User sent a real photo of a Loa Tingee bottom plate via Zalo client; the plate has a printed sticker with model `Tingee 3PRO` and serial `3PRO175459` visible in the photo.

**Console output from `openclaw agent --agent main` (CLI replay of the same inbound URL the bot received in Zalo):**

```
=== REPLY ===
Theo thông tin từ FAQ Tingee, em xin trả lời anh nhé:

Đây là **Loa Tingee 3PRO** với mã thiết bị: **3PRO175459**
- Communication Mode: 4G + Wifi
- Battery Capacity: 2000mAh
- Power Input: 5.0V - 1.0A

Anh chờ em xin team CSKH reset loa giúp ạ.

=== META ===
duration: 5.4s (single LLM call, no image tool round-trip)
contextTokens: 131072
provider: dos-ai/dos-ai
```

**Live Zalo screenshot** (sender = JOY's account, recipient = bot in group "Capypara.AI"): bot replied with the same content above within ~5 seconds of the photo arriving, correctly extracting the serial number "3PRO175459" from the photo without calling any tool. Promoting the photo to a native vision attachment via `buildContext({media: [...]})` is what made this deterministic; before this patch (URL inlined as text in the message body), the same photo got the bot to hallucinate "913D5B" on one turn and "không đọc được rõ" on the next.

**Runtime log excerpt** confirming the media path is now exercised end-to-end (redacted timestamps + chat IDs):

```
[zalouser] [default] inbound message
[routing] resolveAgentRoute: channel=zalouser accountId=default peer=group:REDACTED
[agent/embedded] embedded run start: provider=dos-ai model=dos-ai messageChannel=zalouser
[agent/embedded] [context-diag] pre-prompt: ... promptImages=1 historyImageBlocks=0 ...
[agent/embedded] embedded run prompt end: durationMs=4798
```

Key signal: `promptImages=1`. Before the patch this was always `0` for the same inbound URL even though the URL was present in the user message text.

**Reproduction steps:**

1. Provision an OpenClaw container with `@openclaw/zalouser` + a Zalo personal account paired via QR.
2. Configure a group channel allowlist + agent with vision-capable model (any image-input provider; Qwen3.6 in our case).
3. From another Zalo account, send a photo to the group with `@bot ...` mention.
4. Without this patch: observe `promptImages=0` in the gateway log + either hallucinated content or "I can't read images" reply.
5. With this patch: observe `promptImages=1` + the bot reads the photo content accurately on the first response, no tool call.

## Tests

9 unit tests in `zalo-js.media.test.ts` for `extractInboundMedia` covering:
- Photo detection paths (explicit type, extension fallback, thumb fallback)
- Non-photo content shapes (text, link preview, sticker, audio, PDF) - confirm they return `null`
- Whitespace trimming on URLs
- Case-insensitive extension matching with query strings

## Refs

- openclaw image tool design (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.context.ts` pattern)
- zca-js TAttachmentContent shape (`src/models/Message.ts`)

cc @steipete - happy to iterate on shape if you'd prefer a different abstraction.
target.author: joy
